etymology.description: semantic 糸(糸) (sī, "silk; thread") + phonetic 㡭(㡭) (jì) -> to continue
etymology.interpretation: The character 繼 (jì, "to continue") combines the semantic component 糸(糸) (sī, "silk; thread") with the phonetic component 㡭(㡭) (jì). 糸(糸) represents continuity, like a thread that links pieces together, while 㡭 provides the pronunciation. The notion of continuing follows from the image of a silk thread unspooling or being used to connect parts.

examples.chinese: 他决定继续他的研究(他決定繼續他的研究)。
examples.pinyin: Tā juédìng jìxù tā de yánjiū.
examples.english: He decided to continue his research.

examples.chinese: 下雨后(下雨後)，河水继续上涨(河水繼續上漲)。
examples.english: After the rain, the river continued to rise.

examples.chinese: 她希望学生们能继承老师的精神(她希望學生們能繼承老師的精神)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xīwàng xuéshēngmen néng jìchéng lǎoshī de jīngshén.
examples.english: She hopes the students can continue the teacher's spirit.
